Rogue One: A Star Wars Story (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $533,539,991Details
International Box Office $521,543,605
Worldwide Box Office $1,055,083,596
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $18,651,133
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $69,609,755
Total $88,260,888
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In a time of conflict, a group of unlikely heroes band together on a mission to steal the plans to the Death Star, the Empire’s ultimate weapon of destruction. This key event in the Star Wars timeline brings together ordinary people who choose to do extraordinary things, and in doing so, become part of something greater than themselves.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$155,081,681 (29.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:50.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$280,200,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,157 opening theaters/4,157 max. theaters, 7.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $731,253,979

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 16th, 2016 (Wide) by Walt Disney
December 16th, 2016 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
August 26th, 2022 (Special Engagement) by Walt Disney
International Releases: December 14th, 2016 (Wide) (Turkey)
December 14th, 2016 (Wide) (Netherlands)
December 15th, 2016 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
December 15th, 2016 (Wide) (Denmark)
December 15th, 2016 (Wide) (Bulgaria)
Video Release: March 24th, 2017 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for extended sequences of sci-fi violence and action.
(Rating bulletin 2452 (Cert #50667), 11/23/2016)
Running Time: 134 minutes
Franchise: Star Wars

Genre:Adventure

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

In a time of conflict, a group of unlikely heroes band together on a mission to steal the plans to the Death Star, the Empire’s ultimate weapon of destruction. This key event in the Star Wars timeline brings together ordinary people who choose to do extraordinary things, and in doing so, become part of something greater than themselves.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$155,081,681 (29.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:50.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$280,200,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,157 opening theaters/4,157 max. theaters, 7.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $731,253,979
